trivia_bullets.text: Lazotep is a blue mineral native to the plane of Amonkhet, used by Nicol Bolas to eternalize the dead — coating corpses in layers of the mineral to produce the Dreadhorde, undead warriors whose lifeforce and deeds are preserved in the lazotep shell. The Behemoth is a lazotep-coated zombie hippopotamus, part of the Dreadhorde army Bolas transferred to Ravnica via the Planar Bridge for the War of the Spark. [MTG Salvation: The World of Amonkhet, via WebSearch snippet] [Scryfall type_line: Zombie Hippo]
sources: MTG Salvation: The World of Amonkhet

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text (verbatim): '"I know I should be more concerned. But a big, blue zombie-potamus from beyond the stars? This is what they\'re invading us with?" —Mileva, Boros legionnaire'. Mileva is a named Boros soldier whose disbelieving wit in the face of an otherworldly hippo-zombie reads as black humor from a soldier who has seen everything — a tonal counterpoint to the card's threatening stat line (5/4 for five mana). [Scryfall flavor_text field]

Lazotep Behemoth is a vanilla 5/4 for {4}{B} — no abilities beyond its stat line. Its EDHREC rank of 27582 reflects its status as a bulk vanilla common; it sees essentially no Commander play. In the War of the Spark limited environment it was a reasonable curve-topper for black aggro/midrange but offered no special mechanics. Art by Zezhou Chen. [Scryfall EDHREC rank]

The creature type Zombie Hippo is one of the more unusual tribal combos in Magic — hippos are natively a green creature type (see Pygmy Hippo, River Hippo) but this card puts the Zombie subtype first, signaling that the lazotep zombification overrides the creature's original alignment. The visual result — a cobalt-blue armored behemoth — underscores that the animal's origin is unrecognizable under Bolas's death-mineral. [Scryfall type_line]

related_cards.name: River Hippo
related_cards.note: Baseline green Hippo creature for comparison — shows how Zombie typing displaces green identity
